title: Do not use toast notifications for every system message
summary: AI-generated interfaces use the four-color toast notification (success green, error red, info blue, warning yellow) for every message, regardless of urgency or actionability.
instruction: Use toast notifications only for messages that need immediate, temporary attention. Inline messages, banners, and status indicators are better for persistent or context-relevant information. Not every system event needs a toast.
rationale: When every operation from 'Email sent' to 'Server error' to 'New feature available' produces a toast, the notification channel becomes noise. Users learn to dismiss toasts without reading them. Persistent inline messages and contextual banners communicate without interrupting.

violations: A dashboard showing a toast for every data refresh, every filter change, and every export request.
An app that uses toast for informational messages that do not require user action and disappear too quickly to read.
controls: A settings page using inline success messages next to changed fields rather than toasts.
An app using a persistent banner for important system messages and toasts only for time-sensitive actions.
borderline: An error toast for a failed payment is appropriate — it needs immediate attention and is time-sensitive.

exceptions.description: Time-sensitive errors requiring immediate attention (payment failures, security events). Undo-capable actions where the toast provides the undo entry point.

detector.signalDescription: Find toast-like UI components (fixed-position, auto-dismiss notification elements). Count their density — how many distinct toast types appear? Flag as overuse when toasts are the only feedback mechanism for every operation type.
limitations: Cannot distinguish important toasts from noise without understanding the severity of each message.
Javascript-driven toast systems may not expose all toast types in static HTML.

scenarios: An app where every operation genuinely needs notification (audit logging, compliance).
Accessibility requirements where multiple confirmation channels are needed.
mitigations: Count distinct toast types per page. Flag when 4+ distinct toast types exist (success, error, info, warning) as overuse.

fixturePlan.description: A dashboard with toasts for data refresh, filter change, export request, and error.
Same dashboard using inline feedback for filter changes, toast only for significant operations.
A compliance app where every operation needs a visible confirmation for audit purposes.
